purpose: T+L R1 Probe 1: CPU n-gram memory with 131K buckets
notes: 131K buckets WAY too small. 100% occupancy, 93-97% collision. Need 4M+ buckets.

purpose: T+L R1 Probe 1: CPU n-gram memory with 4M buckets
notes: MODERATE signal. 14.2% bigram hit, 1.5 bpt entropy reduction. Collision still 86% — needs more buckets or better hashing for production.

purpose: T+L R1 Probe 3: GPU MTP D=1 from EDSR step_2000
notes: INITIALLY appeared positive (-8.93% BPT). But matched control (below) shows MTP HURT: control reached 5.1039 vs MTP 5.2328. MTP overhead: +0.13 BPT and -10% throughput.

purpose: Matched control for MTP probe: 1000 more steps from step_2000, NO MTP
notes: CRITICAL: Control BEATS MTP probe. BPT 5.1039 (control) vs 5.2328 (MTP) = MTP adds +0.13 BPT overhead. MTP hurts at 98M scale — validates Meta finding. Throughput also 10% better without MTP (38K vs 34K tok/s).

purpose: T+L R1 Probe 6: Differentiable 3-exit halting controller from EDSR step_2000
notes: FALSIFIED. Control (5.1039) beats halting (5.257) by +0.153 BPT. All exits WORSE than control (exit3: 7.14 vs 6.22, exit7: 5.66 vs 5.20). Throughput 21% slower (29.8K vs 37.7K). Mixture loss + halting overhead hurts at 98M scale. ~17% of tokens routed to earlier exits but quality degraded.

purpose: T+L R2 Probe: Engram-style n-gram memory fusion at layers 1/4/7 from EDSR step_2000
notes: FALSIFIED. Control (5.1039) beats memory (5.237) by +0.133 BPT. Gate barely opened (0.127 vs initial 0.119 = sigmoid(-2)). 5% throughput overhead. Fourth consecutive probe where plain control wins at 98M. Pattern definitive: at 98M, ALL auxiliary modules hurt — even CPU-resident memory with zero-init warm-start.

purpose: Continue plain EDSR control from step 3000 to 5000 with WSD cooldown
notes: Strong improvement from WSD cooldown. BPT 5.1039→4.7252 (-7.4%). Exit-8 at 5000 (4.8141) beats full-depth at 3000 (5.1039). LR decayed from 3e-4 to near-zero. Model is approaching 98M capacity limit at this data budget.

purpose: lm-eval benchmark sweep on control step_3000
notes: Large gaps to Pythia-160M (300B tokens) and SmolLM2-135M (2T tokens). Our model trained on ~2.8B tokens (3000 steps × 512 ctx × ~1800 tok/step). Data gap is 100x+. PIQA and WinoGrande closest to baselines. LAMBADA worst (6.6% vs 35%+ — needs more data for memorization).

purpose: Post-hoc exit threshold calibration on control step_3000

metrics.sweet_spot: p30 entropy threshold: 10% compute savings at +0.013 BPT

notes: STRONG POSITIVE for O5. Post-hoc thresholds give meaningful compute savings with minimal quality loss. 10% of tokens have near-zero entropy at exit-8 and can exit with +0.0008 BPT cost. Even 30% early exit costs only +0.013 BPT. This validates fixed exits + post-hoc calibration as the right strategy for inference efficiency.

purpose: lm-eval benchmark sweep on control step_5000 (partial: 5 of 7 tasks)
notes: CORRECTED TOKEN ACCOUNTING: 5000 steps × 16384 tok/step = 81.92M tokens (NOT 2.8B). All benchmarks improved vs step 3000: HellaSwag +0.14%, PIQA +1.04%, ARC-Easy +1.22%, ARC-Challenge +1.02%, SciQ +3.40%. Missing: lambada_openai, winogrande (timed out or errored). Gap to Pythia-160M is ~3660x in training tokens, making these results impressive per token.

purpose: Fix critical token accounting error across all documentation
notes: Codex T+L R3 identified discrepancy. Previous '~2.8B tokens at step 3000' was wrong by ~57x. Actual: 49.15M tokens. This changes benchmark gap interpretation from ~100x to ~6100x at step 3000, ~3660x at step 5000. The model is FAR more undertrained than previously stated.

purpose: Net2Wider expansion of 98M step_5000 checkpoint to 197M model
notes: Net2Wider duplication 768->1024 + 2 new layers with zero-init residual gates. 196.7M params. Ready for 200M training when GPU is free (after 98M hits step 10000). Needs parity verification via det-eval before training.

purpose: Teacher-guided shard scoring with llama3.2:3b for O4 data efficiency
notes: Teacher scoring complete. Differentiated weights: eli5/poetry/writingprompts upweighted (2.0x), fineweb_s0/openwebmath downweighted (0.5x). Mean weight ~1.0. Ready for A/B test: uniform vs weighted sampling from same 98M parent. Interesting: openwebmath scored LOW — teacher found it less informative than expected, possibly due to notation-heavy content being hard to score via text quality prompts.

purpose: T+L R1 Probe 1: Compare DyT vs RMSNorm normalization on matched 42M models
notes: RMSNorm wins BPT (5.08 vs 5.83, +0.75). DyT converges slower but gap closes from 2.2 to 0.75 by step 5000. DyT kurtosis starts extreme (29.3) but drops to 1.5 — much higher than RMSNorm (1.0). Max activations converge (20.6 vs 21.6). DyT doesn't bound residual stream — only internal activations. Generation: RMSNorm more coherent. Verdict: DyT needs more training time or init tuning at 42M scale.

purpose: T+L R1 Probe 2: Compare TOP auxiliary loss vs plain NTP on matched 42M models
notes: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE. TOP at weight=0.05 with activation at step 200 destroys training at 42M scale. BPT 9.68 vs 5.07 (+4.6 BPT worse). Kurtosis explodes to 99.3 max (vs 1.15). Max activations 134 vs 22. TOP loss magnitude (~20) comparable to CE (~8), creating ~12% gradient contribution that conflicts with NTP. Definitive kill for TOP at this scale/weight. TOP needs much lower weight (0.001) or much later activation (step 2000+) if retried.

purpose: T+L R2 Probe 1: Optimizer/norm comparison (3 variants x 5000 steps)
notes: V2 (AdamW+SS-RMSNorm) WINS: best BPT 4.91, best kurtosis, lowest max_act. SS-RMSNorm matches RMSNorm (+0.08 BPT edge) with fewer params. Muon (lr=0.02) LOSES at 42M: faster early convergence (1.7x at step 1500) but periodic instability (regressions at steps 2000, 3500-4000), final BPT 0.10 worse than V2, max_act 2.7x higher. Muon lr=0.02 too aggressive for 42M scale.

purpose: T+L R3 Probe: Trunk choice — pure transformer vs pure conv vs inter-layer hybrid (3A+9H) at 42M
notes: MAJOR POSITIVE: Inter-layer hybrid (3A+9H) WINS by 0.19 BPT over pure transformer with fewer params AND better activation health. Conv-only loses by +0.70. Hybrid gets best of both worlds: attention for exact retrieval + conv for efficient local mixing. Strongest positive signal for hybrid architecture. Intra-layer parallel (every block has BOTH) expected to be even stronger.

purpose: T+L R4 Probe: Intra-layer parallel hybrid (mean fusion, P blocks) at 42M. k=64 vs k=4, both 9L.
notes: k4 BEATS k64 by 0.15 BPT with dramatically better activation health (kurtosis 0.82 vs 1.45, max_act 18 vs 21). k4 nearly ties inter-layer hybrid (4.80 vs 4.72) with only 9 layers vs 12, and fewer params. Strong evidence: (1) small kernels win in intra-layer blocks, (2) intra-layer is higher quality per-layer, (3) 12L concat-project should beat inter-layer. Validates R4 intuition #2.

purpose: R4 microprobe: kernel sweep (k4/k16/k64) on concat-project + ratio comparison (1:1 vs 2:3)
notes: KEY FINDINGS: (1) In concat, k16 best BPT, k4 best stability, k64 catastrophic kurtosis. (2) 2:3 ratio beats 1:1 by 0.06 BPT at +7% params. (3) ALL concat variants show instability (kurtosis 1.4-5.8). (4) Mean fusion k4 at 9L (4.80) beats best concat at 12L (4.83) with 2.4x better stability. (5) concat_2to3_k16 nearly ties inter-layer hybrid (4.77 vs 4.72).

purpose: MiniPLM difference scoring pilot: 200 windows, 10% corpus sample, Qwen3-1.7B teacher vs Qwen3-0.6B reference
notes: Infrastructure validated. Pipeline works end-to-end on CPU. Teacher consistently has lower NLL than reference (gap 0.26). Source ranking sensible: literature/wiki highest, chat lowest. Ready for full 10% scoring when GPU is free.

purpose: R5 100M promotion gate: 24x512 transformer vs 24x512 HEMD-R5-G normalized additive hybrid
notes: MIXED result. Hybrid wins BPT by 0.11 (passes >=0.10 threshold) but FAILS stability criterion: kurtosis 3.94 vs 1.66 (2.4x worse), max_act 63.6 vs 44.8 (1.4x worse). Normalized additive fusion improved over raw concat but still unstable at 100M scale. Per-branch norm + beta NOT sufficient � need stronger stabilization (e.g., post-fusion norm, gradient clipping on betas, or smaller beta init). Hybrid learns faster early (step 1000-1500) but advantage erodes.

purpose: R6 stabilization mini-gate: R6-F (no beta, SS-RMSNorm) vs R6-S (softmax mixing) vs transformer baseline at 100M scale
notes: BOTH FAIL. R6-F (no beta): BPT 4.78, kurtosis 3.5, max_act 58.5. R6-S (softmax): BPT 4.77, kurtosis 2.6, max_act 55.0. Transformer: BPT 4.67, kurtosis 1.7, max_act 41.6. ALL fail success criteria. Root cause: projected branches (256->512) accumulate magnitude through depth, fused_rms shrinks from 0.36 to 0.08 as branches diverge in direction. β was NOT the main instability source.

purpose: MiniPLM scoring: 500 windows, 10% sample (expanded from 200-window pilot)
notes: Consistent with 200-window pilot. Pipeline validated at 500-window scale. Source ranking stable. Infrastructure ready for full-corpus scoring.

purpose: R7-P sidecar probe: post-fusion SS-RMSNorm hybrids at 100M. 24G all-hybrid + 6G+18A mixed schedule.
notes: 24G: FAIL (BPT 4.91 worse than transformer, kurtosis 2.5 > 2.1 threshold, max_act 75.6). 6G+18A: PARTIAL PASS - BPT 4.686 BEATS transformer by 0.071, kurtosis 2.0 within 2.1 threshold, BUT max_act 58.5 > 52 threshold. Key insight: limiting G-blocks to early layers (1-6) + attention for rest WORKS for learning quality. Conv instability is contained to early layers but activation magnitude still bleeds through. Post-fusion norm delayed instability by ~1000 steps vs R6 variants.

purpose: R7 mainline: P-GQA full-dim hybrid vs transformer at 100M. Last hybrid test before kill rule. transformer_24x512 vs pgqa_24x512 vs pgqa_mixed_8q16a.
notes: P-GQA best BPT 4.729 vs transformer 4.757 = +0.028 improvement, BELOW +0.05 threshold. All variants stable (kurtosis <2.1, max_act <52). Architecture decision: PURE TRANSFORMER LOCKED. Hybrid gain insufficient to justify complexity.

purpose: O4 first decision probe: raw data vs MiniPLM source-level reweighted data on identical pure transformer 24x512. Tests whether data selection improves convergence.
notes: Queued after R7 probes. Success: MiniPLM variant achieves lower BPT at step 5000.

purpose: First RMFD KD probe: 2-arm test (control, single Qwen3-1.7B teacher). Tests whether representation-level KD (state CKA + semantic relational) improves over pure NTP.
notes: COMPLETED. Rep KD = HEAD-START ONLY. Final delta -0.008 BPT (noise). KD led early (delta -0.059 at step 2500) but control caught up during WSD decay phase. Arm 3 ABORTED per Codex. KD arm has elevated kurtosis (17.6 vs 4.8) and max_act (80.5 vs 69.0) — potential stability concern at longer training.

purpose: 15K benchmark gate: control vs mechanism-fixed logit KD. Codex-designed inverted-U alpha (0.10->0.60->0.10->0.0), rising tau (1.5->3.0), confidence gating. Logit-only, no rep. From 5K warm-start. THE decisive test of whether scheduled logit KD persists at 90M:1.7B ratio.
notes: CONTROL ARM COMPLETE. BPT=4.082. WSD S-curve: 12K(4.374)→13K(4.284,-0.090)→14K(4.131,-0.152)→15K(4.082,-0.049). Total WSD drop: -0.292. KD arm STARTED at same timestamp. Teacher: Qwen3-1.7B (3.4GB, 92.6% vocab overlap). Alpha warmup active (0.19 at step 50). Stop rules: non-positive by 6K (vs 4.616), ≤-0.02 by 10K (vs 4.420), ≤-0.015 + lm-eval by 15K (vs 4.082).

purpose: 4-arm KD surface ablation: control vs rep-only(CKA+semantic) vs logit-only(cross-tokenizer ETA) vs rep+logit. Tests which KD surface provides persistent benefit beyond head-start. Total alpha=1.0 for all KD arms.
notes: RUNNING. Arms 1-2 COMPLETE. Arm 2 FINAL: +0.018 WORSE than control (4.516 vs 4.498). Rep KD = HEAD-START ONLY (confirmed 3rd time). Full trajectory: +0.038→-0.002→-0.094→-0.130→+0.004→+0.018. Gap peaked at -0.130 mid-training, collapsed during WSD decay. Control consolidated 1.8x better (-0.329 vs -0.182). Healthier activations (kurtosis 4.5 vs 5.0, max_act 61 vs 77) don't translate to better BPT. Arm 3 (logit_only): ACTIVELY HARMFUL. +0.257@500, +0.184@1000, +0.272@1500, +0.273@2000. Gap plateaued at ~+0.27 — constant penalty, no knowledge transfer. KD loss oscillates 1.6-2.2 without convergence. Cross-tokenizer logit KD at α=1.0/T=2.0/1:19 = constant ~0.28 BPT penalty (not head-start, persistent harm). KD loss oscillated 1.5-2.2 without convergence. Arm 3 FINAL: 4.783 BPT (+0.285 vs control 4.498). Arm 4 @500: BPT=5.169 (+0.147 vs control), kurtosis=2.5 (LOWEST of all arms), max_act=50.4. Logit penalty ~57% of arm 3's (not halved as predicted). BUT stabilization is remarkable — rep KD IS helping activations even if not BPT. Arm 4 @1000: BPT=5.039 (+0.128 vs control 4.911). Penalty narrowed only -0.019 in 500 steps (arm 2 recovered -0.040 in same interval). Kurtosis rose 2.5→3.4 (stabilization advantage LOST). Max_act 50.4→57.9. Rep stabilization was TRANSIENT — only visible in first 500 steps. Combined arm now tracks logit-only penalty pattern with ~50% amplitude. Arm 4 @1500: BPT=4.940 (+0.110 vs control 4.830). Penalty narrowing: +0.147→+0.128→+0.110. Kurtosis 3.9 (control=4.9). Arm 4 @2000: REGRESSION (4.940→4.979). IF spiked to 2.14 (plateau amplification). Arm 4 @2500: Recovery (4.979→4.893), IF=1.26. Gap widened to +0.210 despite lower IF. Kurtosis 5.8. Arm 4 FINAL @3000: BPT=4.651 (+0.153 vs control 4.498). IF=1.01 (additivity restored at low LR). Kurtosis=12.4 (ALARMING — 2.5x control). Full IF trajectory: 0.99→1.41→1.24→2.14→1.26→1.01. Conclusion: multi-surface KD at extreme ratios is toxic (plateau amplification, kurtosis instability). Logit-only with inverted-U alpha confirmed for 15K gate.

purpose: 197M control arm 15K scout. From scratch (step 0). Establishes 197M baseline before 60K gate. Codex Tier 2 approved.
notes: COMPLETE. 197M: d=768, 24L, 12H, ff=2304, SwiGLU, ss_rmsnorm. 196.7M params. BPT 4.047 (vs 90M@15K: 4.082). Modest benchmark gains over 90M: ARC-E +0.5%, WG +1.9%, PIQA +1.0%. HS and PIQA below Codex predictions — need more steps. Capacity utilization LOW at 15K (13 tok/param). Kurtosis spikes at 8K/13K were transient (feature crystallization). 60K gate approved.

purpose: 60K benchmark gate: 197M control vs KD with Qwen3-0.6B (ratio 1:3). Alpha revised 0.45->0.40 per Codex mid-run review.
notes: RUNNING. Control arm at step 45K. BPT=3.958 (new all-time best, 45K checkpoint saved). Slope: -11.4 mBPT/K. 48K prediction: 3.926. WSD starts at 48K. Revised 60K estimate: 3.53-3.57 central. KD arm starts after control completes.

purpose: T+L R6 Probe 1: TAID 6K extension, single-teacher Q0.6, alpha=0.02, tau=2.0, beta 0->1 over 2000 steps
notes: TAID 6K FAILS both gates (3.5742 > 3.5447). BUT: strong WSD recovery (+0.065 gap narrowed to +0.020), kurtosis 2162 vs CE 4292 (50% lower = better O5), exits slightly better. Single-teacher logit KD has ceiling at 6K. CONFIRMS MULTI-TEACHER PIVOT.

purpose: CPU probe: 24x24 CKA heatmap between student and EmbeddingGemma-300M via byte-span pooling
notes: Teacher L13 is universal attractor (best match for 18/24 student layers). Diagonal CKA high L0-17 (>0.79), drops sharply L18-23. Max divergence at L21 (0.347). Student L23 matches teacher L13 at 0.890 but teacher L23 at only 0.589. Complementary signal is in divergence zone L18-23.

purpose: Pre-flight gradient audit: 3 teachers x 64 eval windows, per-channel loss/grad norms/cosines on shared params (L12-23)
notes: CRITICAL: Q_logit grad norm 6.3x CE (dominates at raw scale, manageable at alpha=0.02). LFM state nearly invisible (0.10x CE). LFM-Gemma CONFLICT (cos=-0.104, 50% below -0.10). Decision: drop LFM from first multi-teacher run OR raise alpha significantly. EmbeddingGemma MUST use float32 (float16 produces NaN).

purpose: T+L R7 Ekalavya: 2-teacher multi-surface KD (Qwen logit TAID + EmbeddingGemma semantic Gram)
notes: FAILS Gate 1 (3.5971 > 3.5856) by 0.012 BPT. BEATS Plain KD and CE 6K. Key findings: (1) multi-teacher avoids TAID dead zone at step 1500, (2) WSD decay recovery 0.050 vs TAID 0.040, (3) kurtosis unstable throughout (AMP bug?), (4) AMP autocast fix NOT applied to this run — Gemma semantic may have computed in bf16. Caveats: AMP-fixed re-run could close gap. TAID schedule (beta 0→1) suboptimal per literature (Peng ACL 2025).

purpose: CE-only matched control for multi_2surface_direct_3k (same init, same WSD schedule, no teachers)
notes: CRITICAL: CE-only control BEATS ALL KD variants. Beats multi-2T by 0.021, TAID by 0.009. KD at this scale/warm-start is net-negative overhead. Implications: (1) student has already learned most of what 0.6B teacher offers, (2) KD gradient interference exceeds information value, (3) WSD-alpha may help by removing KD overhead during consolidation phase.

purpose: lm-eval benchmark comparison: CE 60K base vs CE +3K vs TAID 3K vs Multi-2T 3K
notes: ALL variants within ~1% on every benchmark. No meaningful difference. BPT differences (0.02) produce zero benchmark signal. At 197M/60K warm-start/3K continuation, KD from Qwen-0.6B is detectable in BPT but invisible in benchmarks. TAID has marginal raw acc edge on ARC/PIQA/WG but within noise.

purpose: T+L R8 #1: WSD-alpha schedule (alpha ramp 200, hold, linear decay 2400-3000) with 2 teachers (Qwen logit TAID + EmbeddingGemma semantic Gram)
notes: BEST KD variant: 3.5816. Beats TAID (3.5856) by 0.004, beats multi-2T (3.5971) by 0.016, beats plain KD (3.6063) by 0.025. Still loses to CE control (3.5766) by 0.005 — gap shrunk from 0.021 (multi-2T) to 0.005. WSD-alpha schedule dramatically improves KD: alpha decay removes teacher overhead during consolidation. Recovery 0.062 BPT in last 500 steps — best of any variant. Kurtosis excellent at 2500 (110) but spiked at 3000 (1258). AMP fix active for Gemma fp32. Alpha at 0.0 by step 3000 = final steps are pure CE consolidation. Validates Peng et al. WSD-alpha finding. KEY QUESTION: does a matched qlogit-only with same schedule beat this? If so, Gemma adds nothing.

purpose: Codex R8 step 2: Qwen-only matched control with WSD-alpha schedule (no Gemma semantic)
notes: CONFIRMS: Gemma semantic contribution is NOT meaningful. WSD-alpha (3.5816) vs qlogit_only (3.5904) = 0.009 BPT gap, within noise. All KD variants within 0.021 of CE control. CE control STILL wins. CONCLUSION: at 197M/60K warm-start with 0.6B teacher, KD is net-negative regardless of schedule or teacher composition. Schedule (WSD-alpha) helps but does not overcome the fundamental overhead.

purpose: MiniPLM GPU scoring pilot: student (197M, 60K) vs Qwen3-1.7B teacher on 10K windows
notes: CONFIRMS target-density hypothesis. Gap distribution is Gaussian-like (mean=0.38, std=0.44), NOT uniform. Teacher advantage concentrated in specific windows. Some windows have NEGATIVE gap (student > teacher). Top 20% threshold at gap >= 0.68.

purpose: R9 #1: Train CE-only on top-20% teacher-advantaged windows (MiniPLM scoring)
data_ref: shards_miniplm_top20 (171 shards, 5.0M tokens, top 20% of 48863 scored windows)
command: python code/dense_baseline.py --from-ckpt control_197m_60k_step60000.pt --init-from same --shard-dir data/shards_miniplm_top20 --max-steps 3000 --reset-step --run-name miniplm_top20_ce_3k
notes: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE. BPT 3.58->6.16->7.26, ACCELERATING forgetting. Training exclusively on narrow 5M-token curated subset causes total loss of general capabilities. The curated data cycles ~10x through 3K steps. CE on curated data decreases (model memorizes curated set) while general BPT explodes. Killed at step ~2050. Root cause: catastrophic forgetting from exclusive narrow-distribution training. Fix: mixed training (curated + random) or importance-weighted sampling.

purpose: R9 #2: Qwen3-1.7B (9x teacher) + reverse KL + hard 10% tokens + WSD-alpha
notes: FAILED TO BEAT CE CONTROL. Final BPT 3.5934 vs CE 3.5766 (+0.017). Best WSD recovery of any variant (0.077) but starting point too high. 1.7B teacher signal is detectable (best recovery during alpha decay) but overhead during stable LR exceeds benefit. Kurtosis excellent during training (dropped to 196 at step 2500) but spiked to 1611 at step 3000 (same pattern as other WSD runs). Reverse KL on hard tokens is not sufficient to overcome the fundamental issue: online KD from ANY single teacher adds overhead that exceeds information transfer at 3K scale with warm-start.

purpose: R10: Offline sparse replay KD with Q1.7B pre-computed top-64 logits. 85% random CE + 12.5% curated replay. Reverse KL on hard 10% student-entropy tokens. WSD-alpha schedule.
notes: KILLED at step 3000. Gap +0.021 vs CE-matched (3.626) exceeds 0.005 threshold. Offline sparse replay with top-64 cached logits is decisively worse than CE continuation. Root causes: (1) biased gradients from top-K caching (ACL 2025 RSKD proves this), (2) basin-locked warm-start, (3) alpha too low (0.003 KD vs 2.8 CE = 1:1000 ratio), (4) 12.5% replay too sparse. LAST single-teacher warm-start KD experiment.

purpose: R11 Probe 2 v1: Ekalavya-RKP KnowledgePorts + Q1.7B DSKD ETA + reverse KL + SE-KD top-20%. alpha=0.7, LR=3e-4.
notes: KILLED at step 500. alpha=0.7 too aggressive — KD overwhelms CE, eval BPT 0.098 above baseline and oscillating. LR=3e-4 also too high for frozen-bottom fine-tuning. Restarted as v2 with alpha=0.3, LR=1e-4.

purpose: R11 Probe 2 v2: Ekalavya-RKP KnowledgePorts + Q1.7B DSKD ETA + reverse KL + SE-KD top-20%. alpha=0.3, LR=1e-4 (conservative).
notes: KILLED at step ~1250. Noise-level improvement — 0.004 BPT above baseline at step 1000. After 1000 steps with 1.7B teacher, student hasn't even recovered to its starting point. 'Close to baseline' IS failure — world-class teachers should produce fast visible growth. The warm-start logit KD mechanism is fundamentally broken regardless of ports, alpha, or LR tuning.

purpose: From-scratch KD with Q1.7B, alpha=0.9, tau=1.0 (literature rec)
notes: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE. KD 2.15 BPT behind CE-only at step 1000. alpha=0.9 with cross-tok KD drowns CE gradient with noise. Killed at step 1000.

purpose: From-scratch KD with Q1.7B, alpha=0.9, tau=0.5 (Codex R12 design)
notes: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE. tau=0.5 marginally better than tau=1.0 but still 2.29 BPT behind CE-only at step 1000. tau change alone cannot fix cross-tok KD at high alpha. Killed at step 1000.

purpose: From-scratch KD with Q1.7B, alpha=0.3, tau=0.5 — diagnostic for low-alpha cross-tok KD
notes: Appeared to beat CE flat-LR (4.95 vs 5.39) but WSD control proved advantage was entirely from WSD schedule, not KD. Cross-tok KD contributes 0.02 BPT = noise.

purpose: CE-only with WSD schedule — control to isolate KD effect from WSD effect
notes: WSD gives -0.42 BPT free improvement. Matched alpha=0.3 KD at every checkpoint — proving KD adds zero value. KEY CONTROL that killed cross-tok logit KD.

purpose: R13: Hidden-state contrastive KD via byte-span InfoNCE. Vocabulary-independent — bypasses dead cross-tok logit path.
notes: FIRST POSITIVE KD RESULT. -0.113 BPT improvement over CE-WSD at 3K. Gap peaked at -0.312 (step 1000), compressed to -0.036 (step 1500), reopened to -0.266 (step 2000), held through decay. NOT a head-start artifact. However 4.8576 > 4.82 kill threshold from R13 design. Marginal pass — needs Codex evaluation for continue/kill decision.

purpose: R14: Phase-shaped alpha + n_spans=16 hidden-state KD. Codex designed 6-phase alpha schedule to exploit R13 gap oscillation.
notes: KILLED at step 1000. BPT=6.8263 > 6.60 kill threshold. WORSE than CE-WSD control (6.786) AND worse than R13 (6.4738) at same step. Alpha=0.55 sustained for 700 steps (200-900) overwhelmed CE learning. Coarser spans (16 vs 32) didn't help. Diagnosis: R13's alpha_max=0.30 was near-optimal; pushing to 0.55 is destructive. R14's 6-phase schedule was not the issue — the alpha MAGNITUDE was.

purpose: R15: Predictive Coding Distillation from-scratch 6K. Student PREDICTS teacher hidden states via Huber loss with learned precision heads and entropy-based active span selection. FINAL KD mechanism experiment.
notes: Codex R15 design. Huber(delta=0.5) on L2-normalized spans, precision heads (LN+Linear, bias=-2.2), top-20% entropy selection, 4-phase lambda schedule. Kill: 500>7.61, 1000>6.56, 1500>=CE-WSD, 3000>4.80, 6000 ΔBPT>-0.20 vs CE-WSD 6K. Success: 3000<=4.75, 6000 ΔBPT<=-0.25.

purpose: R15: Predictive Coding Distillation from scratch (Huber+precision+entropy gating)
notes: Process died at step 400. Precision heads saturated to ~0.99 (no-op) by step 150. Tracking behind R13. 16th failed KD experiment.

purpose: Diagnostic probes on 60K student: SVD spectrum, inter-layer CKA, exit CKA, entropy, weight analysis, gradient sensitivity, student-teacher CKA
notes: ROOT CAUSE PROBES. Exit crystallization (CKA 0.978 at L15-L23). Student-teacher already 93% aligned. Gradient dead zone (7.8x weaker at L15 vs L0). R13 improvement was regularization, not KD.

purpose: Tokenizer boundary/support audit for logit KD viability
notes: FORMALLY KILLS cross-tokenizer logit KD. Need >95% boundary match, got 72-73%. Teacher-side vocab coverage 9.8%.

purpose: Track 1: VICReg spectral regularization (no teacher) from scratch. Tests if anti-collapse alone matches R13 BPT=4.858.
notes: VICReg var+cov on exits. Beats CE control by 0.056 BPT. Captures ~50% of R13 advantage. Proves ~half of R13 was regularization effect. Kurtosis grew to 10.7 (variance term creates sharp activations). R13 pulled ahead during WSD cooldown. At step 2000, spectral reg matched R13 EXACTLY (5.4593 vs 5.4598).

purpose: Track 1b: Decoupled VICReg with scheduled decay. Var decays [2000,2400]->0, cov decays [2400,3000]->0.001. Tests if kurtosis pathology explains R13 gap.
notes: Kurtosis controlled (1.5 vs 10.7 scalar) but BPT worse than scalar VICReg. Proves kurtosis was NOT the cause of R13 gap. Per Codex decision tree: move to Option A (VICReg + InfoNCE combined).

purpose: Option A: VICReg + InfoNCE combined (tests if anti-collapse + teacher alignment are additive)
notes: SUCCESS (BPT<=4.86 criterion met). VICReg+InfoNCE additive: BPT=4.845 beats R13 (4.858) by 0.013 and CE control (4.970) by 0.125. Kurtosis high (23.0) but not damaging. Effects additive — future KD should always include VICReg.

purpose: Performance Engineer audit for Sutra-Dyad byte model: VRAM accounting, SDPA backend check, throughput timing, and ByteShardedDataset cold-path profiling.
notes: NOT CLEAN. Crash was hardware sizing + allocator pressure, not a leak. Memory-efficient SDPA is active by default; math fallback is catastrophic. Highest-value fixes: fused F.rms_norm, optional global checkpointing, project global context before byte repeat, and remove full-shard byte decode from the training hot path.

purpose: Sutra-Dyad Stage 0: flat-byte baseline scout (Megabyte-style, fixed patch_size=6)
data_ref: shards_bytes (246 shards, 80GB UTF-8 bytes from FineWeb)
notes: Stage 0 scout. BPB 2.187 at 3K steps vs dense baseline equivalent 0.852 BPB at 60K steps. Learning curve healthy: halving gap ~every 1K steps. Generation forming English words by step 1500. WSD decay consolidation effective. VRAM stable at 13.1GB with 11GB headroom. Conversion: 4.283 bytes/token on our data, so 2.187 BPB = ~9.37 equivalent BPT.

purpose: Stage 0 full 10K control run — establish floor for Stage 1 comparison
notes: BPB 6.49 -> 1.725 in 10K steps. Beat Codex projection of 1.7-1.9. Still learning (not plateaued). Generation produces grammatical English fragments. This is the Stage 0 floor — Stage 1 must beat 1.725 BPB.
